length_bias_revision: cycle-2 rebalance — Q1 D1 expanded from 63 to ~127 chars; Q3 D1/D2/D3 expanded, C trimmed from 140 to ~120; Q4 D1/D2/D3 expanded, C trimmed from 218 to ~136; Q5 D3 expanded from 107 to ~131; Q6 D3 expanded from 105 to ~120. All correct-to-distractor-mean ratios now <1.20.

objective_map.q1: LO1 — Explain in plain language what role Python plays in connecting a human specification to an AI system.
objective_map.q2: LO2 — Describe the four-layer model: human intent → specification → Python code → AI API call → output.
objective_map.q3: LO3 — Distinguish between Python as an orchestration layer and Python as a syntax-learning exercise.
objective_map.q4: LO5 — Explain why the AI ecosystem converged on Python as its standard orchestration language.
objective_map.q5: LO4/LO5 — Connect Python's interpreted nature and REPL to the fast-feedback orchestration workflow; supports LO1.
objective_map.q6: LO6 — Recognise that a programmer's primary job is to understand and direct code, not to memorise syntax.

questions.objective_ref: LO1 — Explain in plain language what role Python plays in connecting a human specification to an AI system.
LO2 — Describe the four-layer model: human intent → specification → Python code → AI API call → output.
LO3 — Distinguish between Python as an orchestration layer and Python as a syntax-learning exercise.
LO5 — Explain why the AI ecosystem converged on Python as its standard orchestration language.
LO4/LO5 — Connect Python's interpreted nature and REPL to the fast-feedback workflow; supports LO1.
LO6 — Recognise that a programmer's primary job is to understand and direct code, not to memorise syntax.
